name: Stollen
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large bowl, mix milk and applesauce.
3. Add eggs, almond extract, rum extract, sugar, salt, mace, cardamom, lemon peel, and orange peel to the wet mixture. Mix well.
4. Sift together flour and yeast into another bowl.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring until a soft dough forms.
6. Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for about 10 minutes.
7. Shape the dough into a log and place it in a greased baking dish.
8. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise at room temperature for about 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
9. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
10. Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown and crusty.
11. Remove from oven and allow to cool on a wire rack.
12. Roll out the cooled dough into thin rectangles, about 1/8 inch thick.
13. Arrange raisins, currants, almonds, and candied fruit in a single layer over one half of the dough.
14. Fold the uncovered side of the dough over the toppings and roll tightly to form a log.
15. Place the log seam-side down in a greased baking dish.
16. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise for another 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
17. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
18. Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown and crusty.
19. Remove from oven and allow to cool completely before slicing and serving.
